summary: Landscape title screen now includes a character debug route with mobile pose controls, while preserving the compressed WebP title background, loading asset gate, and debug-exposed phases.
observations: Center metrics HUD includes FPS, XYZ coordinates, JS heap, level triangle count, and chunk count.
Coordinates use the player's debug scene position with one decimal precision.
Playwright smoke checks HUD group overlap and badge text overflow across the declared mobile landscape viewport set.
A compact top-right music mute button is exposed at [data-music-toggle].
The portrait-only rotate prompt now includes a CSS-built phone and rotation icon.
The rotate prompt phone sweeps toward landscape with CSS motion.
The rotate prompt arrow was removed; the phone animation is the only orientation visual.
The bottom weapon preview tray was removed and replaced by a single [data-weapon-cycle-button] beside the fire button.
The weapon cycle button exposes the active weapon through data-active-weapon-id and an updated ARIA label.
Screenshot QA for this pass is stored under artifacts/sub-agents/20260704-weapon-cycle-burst/smoke-qa-agent/.
GPT Image 2 generated three no-text landscape title concepts under artifacts/sub-agents/20260705-title-screen/landscape-title-hud-agent/generated-designs/.
Concept 002 was selected because it leaves the cleanest left-side title/start zone and matches the metallic FPS foundation.
The runtime title background is public/assets/title/sigilbreaker-title-bg.webp and is loaded through publicAssetUrl for assetBuild cache busting.
The loading screen waits for 18 boot assets: 17 gameplay assets plus ui.title.background.sigilbreaker.generated.
The debug snapshot now exposes snapshot.scene.phase and snapshot.ui.loading for title/start QA.
The title screen now exposes [data-title-character-debug] for the player pose harness.
The character debug page uses the existing portrait rotate prompt and disables combat touch zones outside gameplay.
decisions: Portrait should show a rotate prompt.
Reward choices must show clear stat deltas and selected state.
Debug coordinates should stay compact and hidden from final production HUD unless QA mode is active.
Center metrics may wrap, but must not overlap left/right HUD groups.
Music mute should stay an icon-sized HUD control rather than a full text badge.
Portrait prompt must cover the viewport because portrait is not playable.
Weapon switching should stay in the right thumb zone beside held-fire so the bottom center remains clear for the first-person viewmodel.
The rotate prompt should remain CSS-driven and respect prefers-reduced-motion.
The rotate prompt should stay phone-only unless a future screenshot review proves another cue is needed.
Use live DOM title and start button text over generated no-text background art.
Use the condensed sci-fi sans font direction for title and loading UI.
Keep the title and start button anchored left, away from the center-right portal and right thumb combat controls.
Ship the title background as compressed WebP instead of the full generated PNG.
Route debug tools through explicit game phases instead of stacking them over gameplay controls.
caughtIssues: Initial coordinate badge sizing risked crowding the 667 px landscape HUD; CSS now uses auto/minmax/auto grid columns and wrapped center metrics.
HUD safe-area and reward-choice rules are still future work.
Adding more right-side HUD controls can crowd the build, weapon, and ammo badges on 667px landscape viewports.
The bottom weapon tray competed with viewmodel framing and should not return without a new mobile layout review.
The first title screenshot caught the loading overlay ghosting beneath the title; hidden full-screen overlays now use display none.
The selected art needed a stronger left-side title-safe dark gradient so generated background details do not read as stray UI.
The look zone needed to be disabled outside gameplay so the character debug page can receive mobile touches cleanly.
risks: Reward UI can become unreadable if powerup names and stat copy are not constrained early.
Future title or reward UI can become unreadable if placed over the center-right portal detail.

rotatePrompt.visibleWhen: viewport width is less than height

titleScreen.fontDirection: wide condensed sci-fi sans, all caps, squared cuts
